channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Trump tariffs could create a huge headache for streamers
item.description: With Donald Trump set to return to the White House in January, streaming media and consumer electronics companies have to prepare for disruption ahead. Some of Trump’s policies could arguably benefit the industry.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks, once star performers at the entertainment giant
item.description: Comcast is spinning off many of NBCUniversal’s cable television networks, including USA Network, CNBC and MSNBC, into a separate public company.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: Australian communications minister says the government won’t force social media users to hand over personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: iOS 18.1 Update Raises Privacy Concerns: What You Need to Know
item.description: iOS 18.1 update introduces advanced AI capabilities, but also raises concerns about privacy and data protection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azure AI Foundry tools for changes in AI applications
item.description: The way we use artificial intelligence is changing. Chatbots aren’t going away. We’ll continue to use them to deliver basic, natural language, self-service applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE agency may launch a mobile app to file your taxes
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) doesn’t have a staff yet and won’t technically exist for another two months, but the two are already reportedly looking at substantial changes to the government’s status quo.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia’s rivals are focusing on building AI inference chips. Here’s what to know
item.description: Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft Enhances Windows Copilot Runtime with AI-Backed APIs and Generative Model
item.description: Microsoft updates Windows Copilot Runtime with AI-backed APIs for image processing and Phi 3.5 Silica generative model
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Study: Chat GPT is better than doctors at diagnosing illness
item.description: A new research study indicates that Open AI’s chatbot Chat GPT-4 is better at diagnosing diseases than human doctors, according to The New York Times. Fifty doctors, a mix of attending physicians and residents, participated in the study; diagnoses were based on evaluations of medical patient cases.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 12 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says industry was "intensely engaged" in making the new DHS guidelines, and it's up to companies to adopt them. The post DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
